mission.title: external-secrets: (Vault Provider) - Getting multiple secrets does not work if KV store name contains `data` keyword
mission.description: (Vault Provider) - Getting multiple secrets does not work if KV store name contains `data` keyword. This issue affects 20+ users.

steps.title: Identify external-secrets troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your external-secrets deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets -n external-secrets --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review external-secrets configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant external-secrets configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets
kubectl get configmap -n external-secrets -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=external-secrets
```
Hello, 

First of all thank you for creating this project and maintaining it

I'm not able to get multiple secrets from a Vault KV V2 store if the KV name contains `data` keyword (for example: `mykv_data`)

**To Reproduce**

Steps to reproduce the

steps.title: Apply the fix for (Vault Provider) - Getting multiple secrets does not work
steps.description:
## Problem Statement

What is the problem you're trying to solve?
How do you like to solve the issue and why?
```yaml
apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1beta1
kind: ClusterSecretStore
metadata:
  name: myClusterSecretStore
spec:
  provider:
    vault:
      auth:
        ...
      server: https://myvault_fqdn
      version: v2
```

steps.title: Confirm (Vault Provider) - Getting multiple secrets does is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-secrets -n external-secrets --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n external-secrets --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
## Problem Statement

What is the problem you're trying to solve?
How do you like to solve the issue and why?
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1beta1
kind: ClusterSecretStore
metadata:
  name: myClusterSecretStore
spec:
  provider:
    vault:
      auth:
        ...
      server: https://myvault_fqdn
      version: v2

apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1beta1
kind: ExternalSecret
metadata:
  name: list-secrets
spec:
  dataFrom:
  - find:
      path: mykv_data/data/path/to/my/secrets
      name:
        regexp: ".*"
  refreshInterval: 1m
  secretStoreRef:
    kind: ClusterSecretStore
    name: myClusterSecretStore
  target:
    name: list-secrets

* 1 error occurred:
           * permission denied
  Warning  UpdateFailed         2m (x5 over 2m)      external-secrets  cannot read secret data from Vault: Error making API request.

URL: GET https://myvault_fqdn/v1/mykv_metadata/data/path/to/my/secrets?list=true
Code: 403. Errors:

* 1 error occurred:
  * permission denied

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with external-secrets installed or the issue environment reproducible.
